items.html: Define the Transition Navigator Role
Review an example of the the Transition Navigator role
Review resources to support transition planning
Identify ways to measure transition success

data.html:
Supporting Transitions

In lessons 1, and 2, we learned about some of the barriers patient's and families face during the transition from pediatric to adult care.

Support during the transition period can help reduce some of these barriers.

Different transition support models exist, depending on program needs and available resources.

At SickKids and UHN, a Transition Navigator (TN) role has been created

The TN supports patients and families with transition preparation, transfer to adult care, and post-transfer support in the adult care setting.

This Transition model is based on recommendations for transition planning: www.gottransition.org

data.html: What are the responsibilities of Transition Navigator?

Sample Transition Model

To help with visualizing what the TN does, let's walk through a sample model from SickKids and UHN.

In this model, the TN meets with patients and their caregivers starting as early as 12 years of age and is a part of their journey in the paediatric setting until the age of 18.

At first contact with the patients the TN:

items.html: Introduces idea of transitioning between paediatric and adult health care at 18 years of age
Discusses shifting from family-centered to patient-centered care to patients and their parents/caregivers
Encourages teens to become more active in their health care (e.g. booking own appointments)
Collaborates in the development of the skills needed to teens to successfully self-manage their disease
Explores caregiver and family perspectives how to support transition for the patient

data.html: The TN role is described to teen and parents/caregivers, includes information such as:

items.html: Providing education around topics related to transition
Supporting teen in creating goals
Monitoring progress
Learning how to schedule and manage healthcare appointments
Accompanying patient to visits as needed

data.html:
Transitions should be started at age 12 and continued beyond the transition at 18 years of age.

Fix this INCORRECT statement by crossing out two wrong words.

Answer: Transition planning should be started at age 12 and continued beyond the transition at 18 years of age.

Transition Navigator and Ongoing Support

At the age of 17 (typically 2-3 months before the 18th birthday), patients attend specialized paeds/adult transition clinic visit which coincides with their final paediatric clinic visit and their 1st adult clinic visit.

blocks.title: Transition Navigator and Ongoing Support Images

data.html:
Measuring Transition - Some Considerations

Measuring transition can be difficult as results are not clear right away

It is important to track patient outcomes to ensure needs are being met

Some options to track transition outcomes include:

items.html: Patient/family feedback surveys
Appointment attendance
Quality of life surveys
Participating in a quality improvement and/or research studies to review effectiveness of supports provided.

data.question: When transitioning from paediatric to adult healthcare, the focus moves from family-centred care to patient-centred care.
data.feedback_correct: This statement is true. Although patients are at the center of care in the adult setting, many young people still rely on support from their families and caregivers, and this should be encouraged and acknowledged as a strength by the healthcare team.

slides.title: What Do Transition Navigators Help With?

data.html:
Transition planning should start early, around age 12, and support is provided post-transfer to adult care to ensure a seamless transition.

Question: Hey! Can you remind me what transition navigators help with?

Answer: To improve rates of successful transitions, through support, education, and resourcefulness

Transition Resource Guide for Students with Disabilities - www.transitionresourceguide.ca

data.html:
Samantha's Transition

Samantha is almost 18 and about to attend a transition clinic from pediatric to adult care. She is feeling nervous about leaving the pediatric center she has attended since she was born. In addition, she will be moving away to start University in a new city and is worried about managing her health condition while living on her own.

In this scenario, here are some examples of where a TN might be able to support her:

Psychosocial Support:

  • Acknowledge feelings of nervousness with different changes occurring in her life
  • Review her support system within and outside of the healthcare team and review if any additional supports or referrals are needed
  • Screen for mental health concerns and provide referrals to supports
  • Highlight Samantha's strengths
  • Include Samantha's family / caregivers in developing a support plan

Academic Support:

  • Review post-secondary supports she can register for including academic accommodations, and help her with this process
  • Review her school's other support systems such as peer support, academic workshops, counselling, and transition to post-secondary orientation sessions
  • Schedule a check in with Samantha in the near future, to assist with any follow up support from today's meeting

Healthcare Navigation:

  • Review with Samantha and her support system considerations for moving away from the family home and managing her health condition such as locations of local emergency department, pharmacy, and how to contact her health care team
  • Review the process for transition to adult care, so Samantha and her family / caregivers can ask questions about the process
  • Ensure medical team reviews considerations such as signs and symptoms to seek emergency care for her health condition, and information about her medication
  • Ensure Samantha has access to her medical information such as using a health passport ( www.myhealthpassport.ca ) or registering for her care system's electronic patient portal.

items.html: Transition Navigators are a value add to the transition of AYA patients.
Transitions should be started at age 12 and continued beyond the transition at 18 years of age.
Incorporate the use of standardized tools for transition readiness, such as the Transition Readiness Assessment Questionnaire (Brown & Sobota, 2016).
Transition involves more than just healthcare, also includes other life transitions such as transition to university/college, work, etc.

Allemang B, Allan K, Johnson C, Cheong M, Cheung P, Odame I, et al. Impact of a transition program with navigator on loss to follow-up, medication adherence, and appointment attendance in hemoglobinopathies. Pediatr Blood Cancer. 2019 Aug;66(8):e27781.
Allemang BA, Bradley J, Leone R, Henze M. Transitions to Postsecondary Education in Young Adults with Hemoglobinopathies: Perceptions of Patients and Staff. Pediatr Qual Saf. 2020 Oct;5(5):e349
Bradley, J., Lee, Z., & Cheong, M. (2024). Developing a transition workshop for adolescents with sickle cell disease. Science Direct, 2 (2024)
Got Transition. n.d. Retrieved from https://www.gottransition.org/
